homepage_url_justification: Project homepage is the pkgdown site https://bart-turczynski.github.io/sitemapr/ (also linked from DESCRIPTION URL).
sites_https_justification: All project sites are served over HTTPS: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr and https://bart-turczynski.github.io/sitemapr/ .
description_good_justification: DESCRIPTION and README provide a clear description of what sitemapr does: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr#readme
interact_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md explains how to interact with and contribute to the project: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
contribution_requirements_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md states the requirements for acceptable contributions, including tests, formatting, and the verification gate: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
license_location_justification: LICENSE is at the repository top level: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr/blob/main/LICENSE
floss_license_osi_justification: MIT is an OSI-approved open source license: https://opensource.org/license/mit
documentation_basics_justification: Basic documentation is in the README and pkgdown site: https://bart-turczynski.github.io/sitemapr/
documentation_interface_justification: Exported functions are documented with roxygen2 man pages and published on the pkgdown reference index: https://bart-turczynski.github.io/sitemapr/reference/index.html
repo_track_justification: All changes are tracked in git: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr/commits/main
repo_interim_justification: Interim development commits are publicly available on GitHub between releases: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr/commits/main
repo_distributed_justification: Uses git, a distributed version control system: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr
version_unique_justification: Package versions are declared in DESCRIPTION and release tags use unique version identifiers.
version_semver_justification: Uses semantic-style x.y.z versioning in DESCRIPTION.
version_tags_justification: Release preparation workflows are tied to version tags matching v*: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr/blob/main/.github/workflows/full-check.yml
release_notes_vulns_justification: No publicly known vulnerabilities have been fixed to date; NEWS.md records user-facing changes: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr/blob/main/NEWS.md
report_tracker_justification: GitHub Issues serve as the bug tracker: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr/issues
report_process_justification: Users submit bug reports through the GitHub issue tracker: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr/issues
report_responses_justification: Maintainer responds to issue reports on the tracker: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr/issues
enhancement_responses_justification: Maintainer responds to enhancement requests on the tracker: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr/issues
report_archive_justification: GitHub Issues provide a publicly available, searchable archive of reports and responses: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr/issues?q=is%3Aissue
vulnerability_report_process_justificati: The vulnerability-reporting process is published in SECURITY.md and the GitHub security policy: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr/security/policy
vulnerability_report_private_justificati: GitHub private vulnerability reporting provides a confidential channel: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr/security/advisories/new
build_justification: Builds with the standard R toolchain (R CMD build / INSTALL, devtools): https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr
build_common_tools_justification: Uses the standard, widely available R build tooling.
build_floss_tools_justification: R and its toolchain are free/open source software: https://www.r-project.org/
test_justification: Automated test suite using testthat and cucumber: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr/tree/main/tests
test_invocation_justification: Tests run via devtools::test() and as part of R CMD check.
test_most_justification: Coverage is measured with covr and uploaded to Codecov: https://app.codecov.io/gh/bart-turczynski/sitemapr
test_policy_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md requires tests for new features and bug fixes: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
tests_are_added_justification: New functionality ships with tests, enforced in review.
tests_documented_added_justification: The test-addition policy is documented in CONTRIBUTING.md: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
warnings_justification: Warnings are surfaced via R CMD check and lintr in CI: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr/actions/workflows/verify.yml
warnings_fixed_justification: Warnings are treated as errors in the strict R CMD check gate and fixed before release.
warnings_strict_justification: CI sets LINTR_ERROR_ON_LINT=true and the package check uses error-on warning: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr/blob/main/.github/workflows/verify.yml
know_secure_design_justification: sitemapr defensively handles untrusted XML, URL, archive, and network inputs, including XXE-safe XML parsing, bounded archive extraction, and SSRF protections.
know_common_errors_justification: The project explicitly tests common sitemap, XML, URL, encoding, archive, and SSRF failure modes.
crypto_password_storage_justification: sitemapr stores no passwords or credentials.
crypto_random_justification: sitemapr does not generate security-sensitive random values.
crypto_used_network_justification: Network access uses httr2 over HTTPS and validates response handling; sitemapr does not implement TLS itself.
crypto_tls12_justification: TLS is delegated to R/libcurl via httr2, which uses the platform TLS stack for HTTPS.
crypto_certificate_verification_justific: Certificate verification is delegated to R/libcurl via httr2 and is not disabled by sitemapr.
crypto_verification_private_justificatio: sitemapr does not implement custom certificate verification.
delivery_unsigned_justification: Downloads are served over HTTPS by GitHub and GitHub Releases.
vulnerabilities_fixed_60_days_justificat: No outstanding known vulnerabilities; tracker at https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr/issues
static_analysis_justification: lintr, R CMD check, pkgcheck, and dependency vulnerability audits run in GitHub Actions.
static_analysis_common_vulnerabilities_j: R CMD check plus lintr cover common defect classes; OSV and OSS Index audits cover dependency vulnerabilities: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr/actions
static_analysis_fixed_justification: Static-analysis findings are addressed before merge.
static_analysis_often_justification: lintr runs on every CI build (each push and pull request): https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr/blob/main/.github/workflows/verify.yml
dynamic_analysis_justification: Before releases the rhub workflow runs R CMD check, including the test suite, on sanitizer and valgrind containers: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr/blob/main/.github/workflows/rhub.yaml
dynamic_analysis_unsafe_justification: sitemapr is an R package with no compiled code of its own.
dynamic_analysis_enable_assertions_justi: The automated test suite uses testthat expectations and cucumber scenarios, which are checked at runtime during CI.
dynamic_analysis_fixed_justification: Any issues surfaced by sanitizer, valgrind, or R CMD check runs are fixed before release.
test_continuous_integration_justificatio: GitHub Actions verify.yml runs lint, R CMD check, README sync, registry guards, and coverage on every push and pull request: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr/actions/workflows/verify.yml
discussion_justification: GitHub Issues provide a public forum for project discussion: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr/issues
no_leaked_credentials_justification: No credentials are committed to the repository; secret-bearing workflows use GitHub Actions secrets.
english_justification: Documentation and issue-tracker interaction are conducted in English: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr#readme
hardening_justification: Hardening mechanisms such as HTTP security headers apply to web applications; sitemapr is an R library.
sites_password_security_justification: Project sites (GitHub and pkgdown) manage their own authentication; sitemapr operates no site with user passwords.
maintained_justification: Actively maintained: recent commits, issue tracking, and CI workflows are present at https://github.com/bart-turczynski/sitemapr
